title: Multi-step workflow — zero-downtime column type change on large table
migration_snippet:
# Goal: change orders.amount from NUMBER(10,2) to NUMBER(12,4) on 20M-row table
# Current: app reads and writes orders.amount as NUMBER(10,2)
# Target: orders.amount as NUMBER(12,4) for higher precision
expected_feedback: Workflow: column type change requires DBMS_REDEFINITION on EE (direct ALTER MODIFY would rewrite table with exclusive lock). Steps: (1) CAN_REDEF_TABLE check, (2) create interim table with new schema, (3) START_REDEF_TABLE with col_mapping CAST, (4) COPY_TABLE_DEPENDENTS, (5) SYNC_INTERIM_TABLE, (6) FINISH_REDEF_TABLE (brief lock), (7) cleanup + DBMS_STATS. Each phase has rollback via ABORT_REDEF_TABLE.
